content.problemStatement:
**Note**: For the problem KPERM, we have observed that PYPY3 performs better than Python3.

You are given integers NNN, RRR and KKK. Consider all permutations of the integers 111 through NNN with exactly RRR inversions. Find the KKK-th lexicographically smallest among these permutations.

Notes:


The number of inversions in a permutation P1,P2,‚Ä¶,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1‚Äã,P2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äã is the number of pairs of integers (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that 1‚â§i<j‚â§N1 \le i \lt j \le N1‚â§i<j‚â§N and Pi>PjP_i \gt P_jPi‚Äã>Pj‚Äã.
A permutation A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã is lexicographically smaller than a permutation B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äã if there is an index iii (1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N) such that Ai<BiA_i \lt B_iAi‚Äã<Bi‚Äã and for each valid j<ij \lt ij<i, Aj=BjA_j = B_jAj‚Äã=Bj‚Äã.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, RRR and KKK.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:


If the lexicographically KKK-th smallest permutation with RRR inversions does not exist, print a single line containing the integer ‚àí1-1‚àí1.
Otherwise, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers ‚Äï the lexicographically KKK-th smallest permutation with RRR inversions.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: There are two permutations with 111 inversion: [1,3,2][1, 3, 2][1,3,2] and [2,1,3][2, 1, 3][2,1,3]. The first permutation is lexicographically smaller than the second permutation.
